title: MapleStory M Guide for New Maplers
author: MapleStory M GM
summary: An official beginner reference for configuring the interface, using Auto-Battle, growing a character, handling equipment, trading through the Trade Station, and using convenience features.

sections.title: Interface and skill setup
items: The Quick Menu can hold up to eight frequently used menu items and can be rearranged through Menu Edit.
Skill Layout Type A supports up to five skills and Type B supports up to eight skills.
You can save up to three skill presets; switching presets has a five-second cooldown.

sections.title: Auto-Battle essentials
items: Auto-Battle continues while the app is closed and grants a 10% EXP bonus while active.
Potion use thresholds can be adjusted between 70% and 90% to match survivability needs.
Pets can maintain registered buffs, and Fever buffs can be configured for automatic use.

sections.title: Growth and equipment
items: Use daily quests, hunting, and Star Force Fields as the main early progression loop.
Hyper Stats become available from level 141.
Storage, sell filters, and extraction filters help prevent unnecessary inventory handling.

sections.title: Trade Station and convenience features
items: The Trade Station supports item search, filters, buying, and listing items for sale.
A first Trade Permit is obtained through Maple Adventure before using the market.
Maple Guide filters, party search, bundled matching, and quest auto-progress reduce repeated menu work.

notes: Original MPStorys summary and structured facts verified from Nexon's official indexed forum guide on 2026-07-18.
prerequisites: Access to Maple Adventure for the initial Trade Permit
steps: Arrange the Quick Menu and select a skill layout that fits the number of active skills you use.
Configure potion thresholds, pet buffs, and Fever settings before starting Auto-Battle.
Complete daily quests and move into level-appropriate Star Force Fields for growth.
Review equipment before selling or extracting and set filters to protect useful items.
Unlock the Trade Station permit through Maple Adventure, then use filters before buying or listing items.
Use Maple Guide and party matching to find recommended content and group activities.
